calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Default And Default Judgment(Ccp 473(D)) And To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steve Levy, esq. (800-637-6922) for Plaintiff via Court Call.
Robert Schoen, esq. (415-757-4518) of Lega Assistance to the Elderly, Inc. for Defendant.

9:30am: Mr. Levy requests opportunity to argue during second calling of matter as he did not see tentative ruling until this morning. The Court notes Mr. Levy is not in compliance with tentative ruling rule, but will entertain oral arguments.
9:56am: Mr. Levy states he does not have arguments. Mr. Schoen presents a proposed order.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANCISCO RIVAS' Motion To Vacate Void Default And Default Judgment(CCP 473(D)) And To Quash Service Of Summons

Defendant Francisco Varela Rivas's motion to vacate default and default judgment and to quash service of summons is granted. Defendant's motion is brought under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(d), not Section 473.5. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 473(d) ["The Court may, . . on motion of either party after notice to the other party, set aside any void judgment or order"].) It therefore is timely, as "[l]ack of personal jurisdiction renders a judgment (or default) void, and the default may be directly challenged at any time." (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1250; see also Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["A judgment that is void on its face may be set aside at any time."].) A judgment is void on its face when the invalidity is apparent upon an inspection of the judgment-roll, which includes the proof of service. (Calvert v. Al Binali (2018) 29 Cal.App.5th 954, 960-961; Trackman v. Kenney (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 175, 181-183.) Here, the proof of service reflects that the process server purported to effect substituted service on Defendant by leaving copies of the summons and complaint with an unnamed "co occupant." However, substituted service on an individual is not valid unless the summons and complaint "cannot with reasonable diligence be personally delivered to the person to be served." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 415.20(b).) "' Ordinarily, . . . two or three attempts at personal service at a proper place should fully satisfy the requirement of reasonable diligence and allow substituted service to be made.'" (Trackman, 187 Cal.App.4th at 185.) Here, there is no declaration of diligence attached to the proof of service reflecting that any efforts were made to attempt personal service on Mr. Varela Rivas, and the proof of service therefore is facially void, which renders the judgment void as well.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
